## My request for Codex:
this looks fucking fantastic. go ahead and ab-implement this as a new branch and don't stop until the full plan is done.

Implemented on `codex/maintainability-reduction`.

- LOC: **66,594 → 59,435**
- Added: 5,471 lines
- Deleted: 12,630 lines
- Net reduction: **7,159 lines (10.7%)**
- Full Rust, CLI, web, browser-smoke, benchmark, Clippy, build, formatting, contract, and workflow gates passed.
- Real Postgres first-push benchmark passed.
- User-owned `AGENTS.md` change was untouched.
- No commit or push was made.

[View the implementation plan via Tailscale](http://100.96.14.105:8765/2026-07-09-codebase-maintainability-reduction-plan.html)

One closeout limitation: Codex autoreview was attempted three times but rejected by the account usage cap before producing findings. All deterministic checks are green.

::git-create-branch{cwd="/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs" branch="codex/maintainability-reduction"}
ok would you consider this the first pass or is it done?

I’d call it a substantial first pass, not the entire plan.

It achieved the hard LOC target and removed the highest-cost machinery, but these planned items remain:

- Shared API/CLI/web transport-contract owner; CLI still duplicates several DTOs and routes.
- Typed Git OIDs and projection persistence encodings.
- Further replacement of broad persistence locks/aggregate rewrites with focused deltas.
- Migration of API workflow tests behind a narrow integration harness and more shared RAII fixtures.
- Remaining CLI remote/auth-plan consolidation.
- A successful 15-minute autoreview—the three attempts were blocked by the Codex usage cap.

So the branch is green and valuable as a coherent first landing, but I would not honestly mark the full HTML plan done yet.
ok, go ahead with the second pass after you commit and push this branch. don't stop until the rest of the plan is finished

I’m tracing the Clerk sync transaction and its lock ordering first, then I’ll add focused concurrent Postgres regressions around verified-email merging and handle allocation before handing the fix back.

input.input: const r = await tools.shell_command({command:"cat /home/adam-blumoff/.agents/skills/systematic-debugging/SKILL.md && sed -n '1,260p' crates/scope-core/src/db/clerk_users.rs && rg -n \"metadata_lock|advisory|handle|verified_email|clerk\" crates/scope-core/src/db crates/scope-core/migrations -g '*.rs' -g '*.sql'","workdir":"/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs","timeout_ms":10000}); text(r)

const r = await tools.shell_command({command:"sed -n '1,180p' crates/scope-core/src/db/locks.rs; sed -n '260,620p' crates/scope-core/src/db/clerk_users.rs; rg -n \"resolve_clerk_user|ClerkIdentity|scope_users.*email|UNIQUE.*email|idx_scope_users\" crates/scope-core api migrations . -g '*.rs' -g '*.sql' --glob '!target/**' --glob '!cli/target/**'","workdir":"/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs","timeout_ms":10000}); text(r)

const r = await tools.shell_command({command:"sed -n '1,280p' api/tests/workflows/auth.rs; sed -n '1,260p' crates/scope-core/src/db/test_support.rs; sed -n '1,230p' crates/scope-core/src/db/mod.rs; rg -n \"TestDatabase|test_store|test_database|SCOPE_TEST_DATABASE_URL|create.*schema|MetadataStore\" crates/scope-core/tests crates/scope-core/src/db/*tests.rs api/tests/workflows -g '*.rs'","workdir":"/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs","timeout_ms":10000}); text(r)

content.text: The root cause is confirmed: the transaction locks only `clerk:<subject>`, while the two unique decisions are keyed by normalized email and the global handle candidate space. Different subjects therefore read the same “available” values before either commits. I’m fixing those exact serialization boundaries—email first for merge ownership, then a single allocation lock only when creating a new handle.

input.input: const r = await tools.shell_command({command:"rg -n '^edition' Cargo.toml crates/scope-core/Cargo.toml; rustfmt --edition 2024 crates/scope-core/src/db/clerk_users.rs; git diff --check -- crates/scope-core/src/db/clerk_users.rs; SCOPE_TEST_DATABASE_URL=REDACTED cargo test -p scope-core db::clerk_users::tests --locked -- --test-threads=1","workdir":"/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs","timeout_ms":120000}); text(r)

content.text:
Fixed the Clerk sync uniqueness races in `crates/scope-core/src/db/clerk_users.rs`.

- Added normalized-email locking for verified-email merges.
- Added a global handle-allocation lock only for new users.
- Preserved fixed lock ordering: identity → email → handle allocation.
- Added concurrent Postgres regression tests for same-email merging and preferred-handle collisions.
- Focused tests pass repeatedly; strict scope-core clippy passes.
